message:
feat(platform): implement POSIX clock, entropy, and log hooks

Implement the POSIX platform adapter in src/platform/posix/hooks.c with:
- posix_wall_clock: clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC) with REALTIME fallback
- posix_logical_clock: counter-based deterministic clock for replay
- posix_entropy_u64: getrandom(2) primary (Linux), getentropy (macOS),
  /dev/urandom fallback, pid+timestamp last resort
- posix_log_stderr: fprintf to stderr with level names
- asx_posix_hooks_install: single entry point populating all hook families

Create include/asx/platform/posix.h public header.

Compiles clean with -DASX_PROFILE_POSIX -Werror -Wpedantic. CORE profile
build and all tests unaffected. bd-zibt, bd-dvaw closed.

Guarantee Impact Block:
- gs_id: GS-012
- change: New POSIX platform adapter functions. No existing behavior changed.
  CORE profile is unaffected (guarded by #ifdef ASX_PROFILE_POSIX).
- preserved invariants: All existing tests and semantics unchanged. New code
  only activates when building with -DASX_PROFILE_POSIX.
- proof artifacts: src/platform/posix/hooks.c, include/asx/platform/posix.h
- fallback mode: Remove POSIX implementations to restore empty stub.
- deterministic isomorphism note: POSIX hooks only used in live mode. CORE
  deterministic profile continues to use default hooks.
- semantic delta budget: 0
- traceability refs: TRC-SEM-001, TRC-PLATFORM-001
- artifact links: build/obj/platform/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-dl42","title":"Child regions: Track child close and decrement parent child_count","description":"When a child region transitions to CLOSED state, decrement the parent's child_count and remove the child from the parent's children[] array.

## Changes
In src/runtime/lifecycle.c, find where region state becomes CLOSED (likely in the drain/finalize path in quiescence.c or lifecycle.c). Add:
1. If region->parent_id != ASX_INVALID_ID: look up parent slot
2. Scan parent->children[] for this region's id
3. Remove it (shift remaining entries or swap with last)
4. Decrement parent->child_count
5. Handle edge case: parent already closed/freed — log warning but don't crash

## Idempotency
Must be safe if called multiple times (e.g., if close path runs twice due to error recovery). Check child is actually in parent's list before decrementing.

## Files
- src/runtime/lifecycle.c or src/runtime/quiescence.c (modify)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:59:43.942128309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T18:49:09.128289969Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T18:49:09.128031299Z","close_reason":"Completed: child region close now unlinks from parent children[] and decrements child_count safely during FINALIZING->CLOSED; missing-parent case is tolerated and logged; added focused unlink regressions; validated with rch format-check/build/lint/test/conformance, focused test_quiescence, and UBS.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dl42","depends_on_id":"bd-lhk6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:01:47.326778804Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

-{"id":"bd-dvaw","title":"POSIX: Implement entropy hook (getrandom/urandom)","description":"Implement POSIX entropy hook in src/platform/posix/hooks.c.

## What to implement
posix_entropy_u64(void *ctx): generate 8 bytes of entropy. Primary: getrandom(buf, 8, 0) on Linux 3.17+. Fallback: open /dev/urandom, read 8 bytes, close. Memcpy into uint64_t. The hook signature is: uint64_t (*asx_entropy_u64_fn)(void *ctx).

## Feature detection
Use __has_include or check for SYS_getrandom. On macOS, use getentropy() from <sys/random.h>. The fallback path (/dev/urandom) works everywhere.

## Testing
- Generate 1000 values, verify not all identical (basic distribution check)
- Verify no errors returned (getrandom should not fail with 8 bytes)
- Guard test with #ifdef ASX_PROFILE_POSIX

## Files

## Feature detection
Use __has_include or check for SYS_getrandom. On macOS, use getentropy() from <sys/random.h>. The fallback path (/dev/urandom) works everywhere.

## Testing
- Generate 1000 values, verify not all identical (basic distribution check)
- Verify no errors returned (getrandom should not fail with 8 bytes)
- Guard test with #ifdef ASX_PROFILE_POSIX

 {"id": "bd-ekb3", "title": "EPIC: Hardcoded Constants, Stale Comments, and Code Quality Remediation", "description": "Master epic for systematically addressing all hardcoded magic numbers that should be configurable,
stale/misleading comments indicating unfinished code, and related code quality issues discovered
during a comprehensive fresh-eyes audit of the entire asupersync ANSI C codebase.

## Background

A full-project audit was conducted scanning every .c and .h file for:
1. Hardcoded numeric constants in function bodies that should be named #define macros
2. Comments containing "will", "would", "TODO", "FIXME" indicating unfinished or deferred work
3. Stale documentation that no longer matches the actual implementation state

## Scope

This epic covers:
- Converting 4 hardcoded magic numbers to named, #ifndef-guarded compile-time constants
- Updating 2 stale header comments that incorrectly describe code as stubs/future work
- Identifying and documenting items verified as correct (not needing changes)
- Ensuring all changes compile cleanly with -Werror and pass existing test suites

## Architectural Principle

The asx project uses a "resource contracts instead of silent degradation" philosophy (README).
Every operational parameter should be:
1. Named (self-documenting in code)
2. Overridable at compile time (#ifndef guard)
3. Documented with its default value and unit
4. Consistent with the profile/resource-class system

This ensures embedded deployers (R1/R2/R3 resource classes) and specialized profiles
(HFT, AUTOMOTIVE) can tune thresholds without modifying source code.

## Verification

All changes MUST:
- Build cleanly with `make build` (strict -Werror)
- Pass all existing unit tests
- Not change any runtime behavior when using default values
- Be backward-compatible (existing code that doesn't override the macros gets identical behavior)", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:02:45.202825037Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-25T03:16:14.362635656Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-25T03:16:14.362076177Z", "close_reason": "All tasks completed: 4 constants extracted, 2 comments fixed, audit trail documented", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["audit", "code-quality", "constants"]}

-{"id":"bd-zibt","title":"POSIX: Implement clock hooks (clock_gettime CLOCK_MONOTONIC)","description":"Implement POSIX clock hooks in src/platform/posix/hooks.c.

## What to implement
1. posix_wall_clock(void *ctx): call clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &ts), return ts.tv_sec * 1e9 + ts.tv_nsec as uint64_t nanoseconds. The hook signature is: asx_time (*asx_clock_now_ns_fn)(void *ctx).
2. posix_logical_clock(void *ctx): for deterministic mode, delegate to the default logical clock (counter-based). In live mode this is unused; wall clock drives time.

## Context
The default_wall_clock() in src/runtime/hooks.c returns 0 (stub). POSIX adapter replaces this with real monotonic time. The asx_runtime_hooks.clock.now_ns_fn field is what gets populated.

## Testing
- Verify two consecutive calls return non-decreasing values (monotonicity)
- Verify returned value is plausible (within expected nanosecond range)
- Guard test with #ifdef ASX_PROFILE_POSIX

## Files

 {"id":"bd-zt9e","title":"RaptorQ: Comprehensive erasure recovery unit tests","description":"Add thorough unit tests for erasure decoding in tests/unit/raptorq/test_raptorq.c.

## Test cases
1. all_symbols_present: encode+decode with all present → output matches input exactly
2. single_source_loss: drop 1 source symbol, verify recovery succeeds
3. multiple_source_losses: drop up to repair_count source symbols, verify recovery
4. max_recoverable_loss: drop exactly repair_count sources, verify recovery (boundary)
5. unrecoverable_loss: drop repair_count+1 sources → ASX_E_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED
6. repair_symbol_loss: drop repair symbols only (all sources present) → succeeds trivially
7. mixed_loss: drop some source + some repair symbols, verify recovery when possible
8. empty_input: 0 symbols → appropriate error
9. single_symbol: 1 source, 1 repair, drop source → recover from repair
10. round_trip_determinism: same input+erasure pattern always produces same output

## Logging
Each test prints: input size, symbol count, erasure pattern, recovery status, byte-level comparison result. Use ASSERT_TRUE with descriptive messages.

## Parameterization
Test with symbol_size=64 and symbol_size=1024 to cover both small and large symbols. Test with overhead_pct=20 and overhead_pct=50.

## Files
- tests/unit/raptorq/test_raptorq.c (extend)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:00:23.945795990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:07:07.107948886Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:07:07.107683424Z","close_reason":"Completed: expanded the RaptorQ unit suite into a broader erasure matrix covering all-present parameterized cases, repair-only loss, mixed recoverable loss, unrecoverable loss beyond repair capacity, single-symbol recovery, and repeated-run determinism; validated with rch focused RaptorQ build/run, format-check, prior build/lint/test/conformance, and UBS.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zt9e","depends_on_id":"bd-3nny","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:02:25.760157586Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,45 @@
+/*
+ * asx/platform/posix.h — POSIX platform adapter public API
+ *
+ * Provides asx_posix_hooks_install() which populates asx_runtime_hooks
+ * with POSIX-native implementations: monotonic clock (clock_gettime),
+ * entropy (getrandom/urandom), and stderr logging.
+ *
+ * Usage:
+ *   asx_runtime_hooks hooks;
+ *   asx_posix_hooks_install(&hooks);
+ *   asx_runtime_set_hooks(&hooks);
+ *
+ * Requires: -DASX_PROFILE_POSIX at compile time.
+ *
+ * SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
+ */
+
+#ifndef ASX_PLATFORM_POSIX_H
+#define ASX_PLATFORM_POSIX_H
+
+#include <asx/asx_config.h>
+#include <asx/asx_export.h>
+#include <asx/asx_status.h>
+
+#ifdef __cplusplus
+extern "C" {
+#endif
+
+/* Install POSIX-native hooks into the provided hook table.
+ *
+ * Calls asx_runtime_hooks_init() for safe defaults, then overlays:
+ *   - clock: clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC) for wall time
+ *   - entropy: getrandom(2) with /dev/urandom fallback
+ *   - log: stderr fprintf sink
+ *
+ * Reactor and blocking pool hooks are not yet populated (future work).
+ *
+ * Returns ASX_OK on success, ASX_E_INVALID_ARGUMENT if hooks is NULL. */
+ASX_API ASX_MUST_USE asx_status asx_posix_hooks_install(asx_runtime_hooks *hooks);
+
+#ifdef __cplusplus
+}
+#endif
+
+#endif /* ASX_PLATFORM_POSIX_H */
